title: Switch / Match
description: Multi-branch conditional rendering. Renders the first matching branch.
signature_switch: Switch(props: { fallback?: MaybeAccessor<CReactNode>; children: MatchResult<any>[] | MatchResult<any> }): JSXElement
signature_match: Match<T>(props: { when: MaybeAccessor<T | undefined | null | false>; children: CReactNode | ((item: Accessor<NonNullable<T>>) => CReactNode) }): MatchResult<T>
switch_prop_fallback_desc: Rendered when no Match is truthy.
match_prop_when_desc: Condition for this branch. Accepts a value or an accessor.
match_prop_children_desc: Content or callback receiving the truthy value.
severity_intro: The uptime monitor picks a status banner by overall severity: one <0>Match</0> per degraded state, and the <1>fallback</1> covers the healthy case so exactly one banner logs per change.
lifecycle_intro: The page writer renders each generated page through its lifecycle: <0>Switch</0> follows <1>page.state()</1> from writing to ready or failed, and each branch uses callback children so the status line is built lazily.
in_the_wild: See both switches in context in the <0>uptime-monitor walkthrough</0> and the <1>page-writer walkthrough</1>.
